Recent updates in New York City include a fatal crash involving an FDNY firetruck, a vacant house collapse prompting evacuations, and a significant Medicaid spending report highlighting waste. Additionally, the city is addressing housing issues with new legislation and a pause on tax lien sales amid financial concerns.

### Risks & Alerts

- Neighbors evacuate after vacant house collapses in Newark. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimgFBVV95cUxQTXJRSVpFeEJYNGZWdGpPS3kxUk92RGlLdTBleGRKbkJZUU5FdjBVMC1HRkl3OWFSWTFBNXZGR1ZKY1BPekREUG1vUWwzeW9yc3NiNFlhaEgtdHpwd3NwNW9xX3Z4YnFCbGE5T0hhbTkzaW1MbWtNdkk0N1Nqd2k3SzlhUmlQTEplT3FQWkZiOF9HNG1nS1h5WGJR?oc=5)
- Brooklyn chain-reaction crash involving FDNY firetruck leaves 1 dead and several injured. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Seafood sold in NY may be contaminated with Norovirus; consumers advised to check sources. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Pothole season arrives in NYC due to brutal winter; drivers advised to be cautious. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMimgFBVV95cUxOOTNwTGpvU0N0MVFrOWpKWDJ5b1F6Um1rOTdJRTRxR1praURLT0g4bE05WEh1UUJoQ2o5LVRaYWxEOVNMa3BLbHNiVXcyWUI3U19weGdJQUR2RmFqcEpybHM2eVVzblRpMHpLWUhHZGc0S2NkcVRpX0Q1R1hkMHhkV29waTd2TVpIaXcyeUdqUXZkYVZQQ0VGaThR?oc=5)

### Civics & Politics

- City Council advances Bronx lawmaker’s bill legalizing basement apartments and tiny houses. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Lawmakers advance bill aimed at lowering housing prices in NYC. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- NYC tax lien sale paused for at least 6 months by Mayor Zohran Mamdani. [↗](https://news.google.?oc=5)
- Mamdani administration restarts homeless encampment sweeps at 11 NYC sites. [↗](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiowFBVV95cUxQbVZGQnZmR0hNV19iWVVzZ09UVlc5TVpUeUxUZmRrRkxsRE5QelZnUEZsZS1zRk54UWtVY3hxUTNyVXI5V2pWekZfMHRZVlhQVGRWNUFJaTAtR2ZqODBzcTVSVkx1TlRJclV4NTF1TXlkbnc5Z0QtSlV3amN1LS15S203b0RndU5GX0szTWcwQlhkUm1qWHhXajBoYi1DZ2Jka2w4?oc=5)
- Moody’s downgrades NYC financial outlook to ‘negative’ due to budget deficit. ### Opportunities & Welfare

- Governor Hochul announces completion of $86 million affordable and supportive housing development in the Bronx. - St. Patrick’s Day Parade in NYC scheduled for March 17; expect street closures and large crowds.
